enrichment.summary: Kyle reviews the 2012 film Prometheus, criticizing it for following the tired Hollywood trope of aliens as one-dimensional murderous villains rather than imagining more advanced or benevolent beings. He contrasts this with his own preferred worldview involving positive aliens, dimensional ascension, and ancient civilizations, then goes on an extended tangent covering Atlantis, the Irish Book of Invasions, mitochondrial DNA research, Cro-Magnon humans, oral mythology, and parallel universes.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Lazy and uncreative — aliens are just evil humans in alien bodies, a dead-end formula that kills optimism
Prefers imagining faultless, advanced aliens who help humanity rather than villains
Believes Plato's Atlantis was real and locates it at the Andros Atoll / Bimini area based on matching dimensions and underwater features
Primary sources should be read directly by everyone rather than filtered through historians
Cro-Magnon had bigger brains and were more well-rounded; modern humans got comparatively stupider due to agricultural specialization
Believes aliens or advanced past civilizations provided technology, including possibly modern computing from Roswell
Sees them as deliberately stirring chaos to fulfill Biblical prophecy, which he views negatively
Impressed by how accurately oral traditions preserve real events across thousands of years
